instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Types of Term Insurance Payment Options
What Is a Term Insurance Premium?
A term insurance policy is a contract between the insurance company and the policyholder. Under the contract, the insurance company agrees to cover the risk of premature demise of the life insured. This coverage is provided against a consideration which is called the premium1.
In other words, the premium is the amount that the policyholder pays to secure insurance coverage under the term insurance policy. The premium is paid over a chosen premium payment term and at a chosen payment frequency (annually, half-yearly, monthly or quarterly).
How to Pay a Term Insurance Premium Virtually?
Thanks to the popularity and availability of the internet, digital payment modes are available which allow you to pay the premium online from anywhere and at any time. There are different ways of paying the premium virtually. Some of them are as follows –
Debit cards
Credit cards
NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer)
RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement
Net banking
Mobile wallets
UPI (Unified Payments Interface)
Pay later facility, etc.
You can check the online payment modes allowed by your insurance company and choose the one which suits you.
How to Pay a Term Insurance Premium Offline?
Paying offline is another type of term insurance payment option. To pay offline, you have to visit the branch office of the insurance company or use the services of an insurance intermediary.
Offline premium payment can be done through cash, cheque, demand drafts, pay orders and other modes accepted by the insurance company.
Types of Term Insurance Premium Payment Options in India
As mentioned earlier, you can choose the premium payment mode to pay the term insurance premiums suiting your budget and affordability. Some of the common types of term insurance payment options are as follows -
Regular premium payment
Under regular premium payment, you pay the premium throughout the policy tenure. For instance, if you choose a policy tenure of 20 years, you have to pay the premium for 20 years.
Limited premium payment
Limited premium payment means paying the premium for a limited tenure which is lower than the policy tenure. For instance, if the term of the plan is 20 years, and you pay premiums for 12 years, it is an example of limited premium payment.
Single premium payment
Single premium payment means paying the premium once, in a lump sum, when you buy the policy. Thereafter, there’s no premium payment liability and you can enjoy the coverage for the chosen tenure.
Which Type of Term Insurance Premium Payment Option Suits You?
The choice of the type of term insurance payment option depends on your affordability and budget. If you don’t mind paying premiums over the entire policy duration, you can choose the regular premium payment option.
For those who expect their source of income to dry up during the policy tenure or if they wish to pay the premium for a limited time, the limited premium payment option is suitable. For instance, if you are nearing retirement age and do not want to pay the premium after retirement, you can choose the limited premium payment term.
For others looking to pay the premium once or those who have a lump sum savings at their disposal, the single premium payment option would be suitable. They can pay the premium once and enjoy the coverage for a long-term period.
Conclusion
Assess your financial suitability and choose the right type of term insurance payment option. Pay the premium within the due dates to enjoy complete coverage against the risk of premature demise. If you are unable to pay the premium on the due date, you can use the grace period to pay the premium without lapsing the policy. Even if the policy lapses, there’s a revival period during which you can pay the outstanding premium and resume the coverage of the term plan. So, pay the premiums of the term plan and maintain the coverage.
FAQs
Where can I make a payment for my insurance premium?
You can make a payment of your insurance premium to the insurance company online or offline through the different premium payment channels offered by the company.
What will happen if I do not pay the renewal premium by the due date?
If you do not pay the renewal premium by the due date, you will get a grace period for the payment of the premium. If you do not pay the premium even during the grace period, the coverage will lapse. Usually, a lapsed policy does not offer any coverage against the risk of premature demise. You can then pay the due premium with additionally charges if any, charged by the insurer and revive your policy.
Reference
https://www.insuranceinstituteofindia.com/downloads/IC38/ALEnglish.pdf (Page 80)
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
